docs.title: Ghostbit
docs.text:
Self-hosted, end-to-end encrypted paste service.

All content is encrypted in your browser before being sent to the server. The server stores ciphertext only — it can never read your pastes.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
# 1. Generate an encryption key
python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

# 2. Configure and start
cp .env.example .env   # fill in ENCRYPTION_KEY
docker compose up -d

# 3. Open http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Install the CLI
docs.text:
pip install ghostbit-cli

cat file.py | gb
gb secrets.env --burn --expires 3600
echo "hello" | gb --password mysecret

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Feature Description True E2E encryption AES-256-GCM, key lives in URL fragment — never sent to server Password protection PBKDF2-SHA256 (600k iterations) key derivation, client-side Burn after read Deleted permanently after the first view Max views Automatically deleted after N reads Expiration TTL from 5 minutes to 1 year Webhook POST notification each time a paste is viewed Language detection Auto-detected from content or file extension Markdown preview Rendered in-browser for markdown pastes CLI gb command — pipe anything, works with scripts REST API Full API for automation and integrations SQLite / Redis Two storage backends, swap with an env var

docs.title: API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://your-instance.com/api/v1

Interactive docs are available at /api on any running instance.

docs.title: POST /pastes
docs.text:
Create a paste. Content must be pre-encrypted client-side (AES-256-GCM).

POST /api/v1/pastes
Content-Type: application/json

docs.title: Request body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description content string Yes Base64 AES-256-GCM ciphertext nonce string Yes Base64 12-byte GCM nonce kdf_salt string No Base64 PBKDF2 salt — present only for password-protected pastes language string No Language hint for syntax highlighting expires_in integer No TTL in seconds burn boolean No Delete after first view. Default: false max_views integer No Delete after N views (≥ 1) webhook_url string No URL to POST on each read

docs.title: Response 201
docs.text:
{
  "id": "aB3kZx9m",
  "url": "https://your-instance.com/aB3kZx9m",
  "delete_token": "rA8mXvLqP2wKjN5sYtUcFg",
  "expires_at": 1716000000,
  "burn": false,
  "max_views": null
}

Save the delete token

The delete_token is shown only once. Append it to the URL fragment to enable the delete button in the UI.

docs.title: GET /pastes/{id}
docs.text:
Fetch a paste. This counts as a view — burn and max_views are evaluated server-side.

GET /api/v1/pastes/{id}

docs.title: Response 200
docs.text:
{
  "id": "aB3kZx9m",
  "content": "<base64 ciphertext>",
  "nonce": "<base64 nonce>",
  "kdf_salt": null,
  "language": "python",
  "created_at": 1715900000,
  "expires_at": 1716000000,
  "burn": false,
  "max_views": 5,
  "view_count": 2,
  "has_password": false
}

docs.title: Errors
docs.text: Code Reason 404 Paste not found, expired, or already burned

docs.title: DELETE /pastes/{id}
docs.text:
Delete a paste using the token issued at creation.

DELETE /api/v1/pastes/{id}
X-Delete-Token: rA8mXvLqP2wKjN5sYtUcFg

docs.title: Response
docs.text: Code Meaning 204 Deleted 403 Invalid token 422 Missing X-Delete-Token header

docs.title: POST /detect
docs.text:
Detect the language of a plaintext snippet. Used by the UI for auto-detection.

POST /api/v1/detect
Content-Type: application/json

{ "content": "import os\nimport sys\n..." }

docs.title: Response 200
docs.text:
{ "language": "python" }

Returns null if the snippet is too short or the language cannot be determined.

docs.title: Webhook payload
docs.text:
When webhook_url is set, Ghostbit sends a POST request on each view:

{
  "event": "paste.read",
  "paste_id": "aB3kZx9m",
  "view_count": 3,
  "burned": false,
  "timestamp": 1715900123
}

The request has a 5-second timeout and is fire-and-forget (failures are not retried).

docs.title: Example — create and read with curl
docs.text:
# Encrypt client-side first (or use the CLI: gb --json)
gb file.py --json
# {
#   "id": "aB3kZx9m",
#   "full_url": "https://…/aB3kZx9m#KEY~DELETE_TOKEN",
#   …
# }

# Delete
curl -X DELETE https://your-instance.com/api/v1/pastes/aB3kZx9m \
  -H "X-Delete-Token: rA8mXvLqP2wKjN5sYtUcFg"

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install ghostbit-cli

This installs the gb command.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
# Set your self-hosted server
gb config set server https://paste.example.com

# Show current config
gb config show

# Remove a key
gb config unset server

Config is stored at ~/.config/ghostbit.toml.

docs.title: From stdin
docs.text:
cat main.py | gb
echo "hello world" | gb
git diff HEAD~1 | gb

docs.title: From a file
docs.text:
gb main.py
gb secrets.env

Language is auto-detected from the file extension.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Short Description --lang LANG -l Language hint (python, go, rust, sql, …) --expires SECONDS -e TTL in seconds. 3600 = 1h, 86400 = 1d --burn -b Delete after the first view --max-views N -m Delete after N views --password PASS -p Encrypt with a password (PBKDF2, client-side) --server URL -s Override server URL for this invocation only --quiet -q Print URL only (useful in scripts) --json Print full JSON response including full_url

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Burn after read
cat deploy.sh | gb --burn

# Expire in 1 hour, max 3 views
gb config.yml --expires 3600 --max-views 3

# Password protected
echo "db_password=s3cr3t" | gb --password mysecret

# Language override
curl api.example.com/data | gb --lang json

# Scripting: URL only
URL=$(cat file.py | gb --quiet)
echo "Paste created: $URL"

# Full JSON response
cat data.json | gb --json

docs.title: Supported language extensions
docs.text:
Auto-detected from file extension when using gb <file>:

Extension Language .py python .js javascript .ts typescript .go go .rs rust .rb ruby .sh, .bash, .zsh bash .html html .css css .sql sql .json json .yaml, .yml yaml .toml toml .md markdown .diff, .patch diff .java java .c c .cpp cpp .php php

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All configuration is done via environment variables (or a .env file at the project root).

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Variable Default Required Description ENCRYPTION_KEY — Yes Hex-encoded 32-byte server key. Used to hash delete tokens. STORAGE_BACKEND sqlite No sqlite or redis SQLITE_PATH ghostbit.db No Path to the SQLite database file REDIS_URL redis://localhost:6379 No Redis connection URL MAX_PASTE_SIZE 524288 No Maximum paste size in bytes (default: 512 KB)

docs.title: Generating an encryption key
docs.text:
python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

The app refuses to start without ENCRYPTION_KEY.

docs.title: Storage backends
docs.text:
SQLiteRedis

Default backend, no extra dependencies. An hourly cleanup task removes expired pastes.

STORAGE_BACKEND=sqlite
SQLITE_PATH=/data/ghostbit.db

Better for high-traffic instances. TTL is handled natively by Redis EXPIRE.

STORAGE_BACKEND=redis
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379

Start with the Redis profile:

STORAGE_BACKEND=redis docker compose --profile redis up -d

docs.title: .env example
docs.text:
ENCRYPTION_KEY=your_hex_encoded_32_byte_key_here
STORAGE_BACKEND=sqlite
SQLITE_PATH=/data/ghostbit.db
MAX_PASTE_SIZE=524288

docs.title: Encryption
docs.text: Ghostbit uses true end-to-end encryption: all encryption and decryption happens in the client (browser or CLI). The server stores ciphertext only and can never read paste content.

docs.title: Algorithm
docs.text: Component Value Cipher AES-256-GCM Key size 256 bits Nonce 12 bytes (random, per paste) Auth tag 128 bits (included in ciphertext) KDF (password) PBKDF2-SHA256, 600 000 iterations KDF salt 16 bytes (random, per paste)

docs.title: Key management
docs.text:
No passwordWith password

A random 256-bit key is generated by crypto.subtle.generateKey() (browser) or os.urandom(32) (CLI).

The key is never sent to the server. It lives exclusively in the URL fragment:

https://your-instance.com/aB3kZx9m#KEY_B64URL~DELETE_TOKEN

The #fragment is never transmitted in HTTP requests — it stays in the browser.

The key is derived from the user's password using PBKDF2-SHA256 (600 000 iterations). A random 16-byte salt is generated per paste and stored server-side (it is not secret).

The URL fragment contains only the delete token:

https://your-instance.com/aB3kZx9m#~DELETE_TOKEN

The password is never sent to the server.

docs.title: Fragment format
docs.text:
KEY_B64URL~DELETE_TOKEN     ← no password
~DELETE_TOKEN               ← password-protected
  • KEY_B64URL — base64url-encoded AES-256-GCM key (no padding)
  • DELETE_TOKEN — raw delete token (shown once, used to enable the delete button)

The server stores a SHA-256 hash of the delete token, never the raw value.

docs.title: Threat model
docs.text:
Threat Protected? Server compromise Yes — server only has ciphertext Database leak Yes — ciphertext without the key is useless Network interception (HTTPS) Yes — key is in fragment, never transmitted Malicious server operator Yes — server can't decrypt URL shared with wrong person No — the key is in the URL Weak password Depends on the user

Secure Context

crypto.subtle requires a Secure Context (HTTPS or localhost). Ghostbit automatically redirects 127.0.0.1 → localhost for local development.

docs.title: CLI encryption
docs.text:
The CLI (gb) mirrors the browser encryption using Python's cryptography library:

  • AESGCM for AES-256-GCM
  • PBKDF2HMAC with SHA-256 for password derivation
  • os.urandom for key and nonce generation

The ciphertext format is identical — pastes created by the CLI can be decrypted in the browser and vice versa.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Docker + Docker Compose — recommended
  • Or Python 3.10+ for a manual install

docs.title: Docker (recommended)
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/stackopshq/ghostbit
cd ghostbit
cp .env.example .env

Edit .env and set ENCRYPTION_KEY:

# Generate a secure key
python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

# Start with SQLite (default)
docker compose up -d

# Start with Redis
STORAGE_BACKEND=redis docker compose --profile redis up -d

The server is available at http://localhost:8000.

docs.title: Manual install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/stackopshq/ghostbit
cd ghostbit
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env   # fill in ENCRYPTION_KEY
uvicorn main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0

Use --host 0.0.0.0

The Web Crypto API (crypto.subtle) requires a Secure Context. Browsers grant it to localhost but not 127.0.0.1. The --host 0.0.0.0 flag ensures you can reach the app via localhost.

docs.title: Install the CLI
docs.text:
pip install ghostbit-cli

Configure it to point to your instance:

gb config set server https://paste.example.com
gb config show

Then paste anything:

cat main.py | gb

docs.title: SQLite
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/stackopshq/ghostbit
cd ghostbit
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env: set ENCRYPTION_KEY
docker compose up -d

docs.title: Redis
docs.text:
STORAGE_BACKEND=redis docker compose --profile redis up -d

Redis data is persisted via AOF + RDB snapshots on a named Docker volume.

docs.title: Nginx reverse proxy
docs.text:
server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name paste.example.com;

    ssl_certificate     /etc/letsencrypt/live/paste.example.com/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/paste.example.com/privkey.pem;

    client_max_body_size 10M;

    location / {
        proxy_pass         http://127.0.0.1:8000;
        proxy_set_header   Host $host;
        proxy_set_header   X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header   X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header   X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
}

# Redirect HTTP → HTTPS
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name paste.example.com;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

HTTPS is required in production

crypto.subtle (Web Crypto API) requires a Secure Context. Without HTTPS, the browser will refuse to encrypt pastes.

docs.title: Caddy reverse proxy
docs.text:
paste.example.com {
    reverse_proxy localhost:8000
}

Caddy handles HTTPS automatically via Let's Encrypt.

docs.title: Data persistence
docs.text: Backend Persistence SQLite Named Docker volume ghostbit_data mounted at /data Redis Named Docker volume redis_data, AOF + RDB snapshots

docs.title: Backup SQLite
docs.text:
docker compose exec ghostbit sqlite3 /data/ghostbit.db ".backup /data/ghostbit.db.bak"
docker compose cp ghostbit:/data/ghostbit.db.bak ./backup.db

docs.title: Backup Redis
docs.text:
docker compose exec redis redis-cli BGSAVE
docker compose cp redis:/data/dump.rdb ./dump.rdb

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
git pull
docker compose up -d --build

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text:
• No IP addresses or User-Agent strings are ever logged
  • Paste IDs are secrets.token_urlsafe(6) — random, non-sequential
  • Burn-after-read fires only on API reads, not on HTML page loads
  • The server never sees plaintext or passwords
